title: The One Where Chandler Crosses a Line

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, everyone except Joey and Chandler are there eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering in a bathrobe) I just walked in the bathroom and saw Kathy naked! It was like torture!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know if we ever go to war and you're captured, you're in for a big surprise.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It just keeps getting worse and worse! Y'know? I mean it's bad enough that I'm in love with my roommates girlfriend-which by the way, I think she knows. Because every time we're in the room together there's this weird like energy between us. And call me crazy, but I think she likes me too. And now I have seen her naked. I mean at least when I've seen her with clothes on, I could imagine her body was like covered in boles or something. But there are no boles, she's smooth! Smooth! (leaves)
Pause

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! Could everyone totally see up his robe?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah! Oh my God!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Joey is getting a phone number from a woman (Casey) as Chandler watches from the doorway.

lines.speaker: Casey
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great! All right, so I'll call you later.

lines.speaker: Casey
lines.text: Great! (leaves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (rushing up) Hey-Hey-Hey! Who was that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That would be Casey. We're going out tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Goin' out, huh? Wow! Wow! (Does a little celebration dance) So things didn't work out with Kathy, huh? Bummer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, things are fine with Kathy. I'm having a late dinner with her tonight, right after my early dinner with Casey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (shocked) What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah. And the craziest thing is that I just ate a whole pizza by myself! (Laughs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait! You're going out with Kathy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Why are you getting so upset?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm upset-for you. I mean, having sex with an endless line of beautiful women must be very unfulfilling for you. (He can't believe he just sad that.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is the big deal? It's not like we're exclusive.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, Joey, Kathy is clearly not fulfilling your emotional needs. But Casey, I mean granted I only saw the back of her head, but I got this sense that she's-she's smart, and funny, and gets you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got all that from the back of her head?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right look, I think it's time for you to settle down. Y'know? Make a choice, pick a lane.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who's Elaine?
Monica and Rachel's, Ross and Monica are sitting on the couch playing cards, and Phoebe is working on a new song.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (singing) "Little, tiny Tarzan, swinging on a nose hair. Swinging with the greatest of ease..." Darn it! Now, I don't know who to get to the next verse.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you could just go uh, "greatest of ease... (plays air guitar) BAH-bah-bha-bhannn." Then go right into it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, ooh, I like that! Yeah. Wait! How do you know about 'bah-bah-bha-bhan?'

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well umm, y'know, I used to play.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, that's right, the keyboards, huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, just a little in high school, but then I really got into it in college. I mean that's-that's when I really found my sound.
Monica is taking a drink as Ross says that, laughs, and snorts her drink.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God! Orange juice just came out of my nose, but it was totally worth it. Oh my God, I completely forgot about your sound.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He used to lock himself in the basement for hours. No one was every allowed to hear, "The Sound."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I wanna hear "The Sound."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really? No. I mean, nah, I haven't played in so long, and-and, well it's-it's really personal stuff, y'know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Come on, play that funky music white boy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, you guys, I mean my keyboards are all the way up in-No, yeah, okay. (Runs out.)
[Cut to later that same day, Ross has retrieved his keyboard and is about to debut, "The Sound."]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! Bring it on, you...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here we go. (Plays one note) Y'know, I've-I've never played my stuff for anyone before, so it's important that-that you understand it's about communicating very private emotions. (Plays another note) Y'know, umm, you should-you should think of umm, my work as wordless sound poems. That's what I'm...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting) Oh my God! Play!

Ross starts to play. He plays a key that has a back beat sound attached to it. Over the background music he plays the sound of a barking dog, a mooing cow, a laser beam, someone coughing, a jackhammer, a doorbell, a police siren, a ray gun, breaking dishes, and for a closer he plays the sound of a loud crash. Basically, the music sucks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (At a loss for words) Boy, that was-that was, umm... terrific.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really, bitchin'!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, it was so-wow!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?! I mean, really?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I mean, you should play in public!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Thanks, you guys. That's uh-ohhh, I wanna, I wanna play you another piece! Umm! Uh-oh! I left my uh, helicopter sounds on another disk. I'll be right back! Okay? (Runs to fetch them) This is so nice, I'm-I am so... (starts to break up and leaves)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God bless my dad sound proofing the basement!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I can't believe I ever let him touch me with those fingers.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What are you guys talking about, I loved it! It was soo moving. Oh, plus it's just, it's so different from the stuff you usually hear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean like, music?
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is watching Yasmein Bleeth running on TV, and the duck starts quacking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know what you're thinkin'! Yes, yes, your breasts are just as firm and juicy.
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come in!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: (entering) Hey! (sees what's on TV) Oh God, is that Baywatch?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh yes, but uh, I just watch it for the articles.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: So is Joey around?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no, he's not back yet, but he'll be here any minute. So uh, come on in. Have a seat. Bow or stern?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: I uh, don't really have a preference. You?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I like it in the stern. (Realizes what he just said.) ...of the boat. (The phone rings, and he answers it.) (on phone) Hello.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on the other end at a pay phone) Hey, it's me. Listen Casey and I were on our way back and had a little car trouble.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We broke down on the Parkway, so I have to walk back and get some transmission fluid. And hey, listen could you please tell Kathy that I'll be there as soon as I can.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why can't you tell her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Cause I only have one quarter, and I think my time is about to-(he stops talking suddenly)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey! Joey!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I thought your time ran out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too, but I guess I do have a couple of more-(his time runs out for real)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Kathy) Uhh, that was Joey. He's running a little late, he says he's sorry.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So I guess it's just uh, you and me then.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I think it is!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: So what did you do today?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I had an appointment to get my haircut...

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: (interrupting) Oh, it looks great!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...and then it got canceled.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Well, I could cut it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?! You do that?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Yeah, I do. Of course, I learned at my aunt's dog grooming shop, but hey, what do you say?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dog grooming huh? Okay, just don't make my tail too poofy.
[Cut to later, Kathy is cutting Chandler's hair.]

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: You have really great hair.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, thanks. I grow it myself. (Kathy is running her fingers through his hair, and Chandler catches himself enjoying it too much.) Y'know who also has great hair is Joey!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Yes! Yeah! Joey has great hair! Umm, I'm basically done here. Just let me get this off your neck.
Kathy leans in really close and Chandler mouths "Oh my God." She moves around in front of him and kneels at his feet.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What-what 'cha doin'?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Checking to see if it's even.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 'Kay.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Looks good.
They lean in to kiss and are interrupted by the phone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (jumping up to answer the phone) Oh the phone! The phone's making sounds! (On phone) Hello!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on phone) Hey dude, it's me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey it's Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen uh, I'm really sorry, it looks like I'm gonna be stuck here for a while. I got the transmission fluid, but when I went to put it in the car, the transmission wasn't there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it must've fallen out a few blocks back. I just figured we hit a dog.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen uh, could you put Kathy on, I wanna apologize.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah man. (to Kathy) Joey. (Hands her the phone.)

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: (on phone) Hey. (listens) Oh no it's fine, don't worry about it. (listens) Yeah-no, stop apologizing, it's okay. (listens) Yeah! I'll talk to you tomorrow. (hangs up) (to Chandler) I should uh, probably go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. Yes! Yeah.
Kathy leaves and Chandler groans in agony. Kathy knocks on the door and Chandler opens it.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: I forgot my purse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh.
They kiss, passionately.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: No, I really did forget my purse.
They kiss again.
Commercial Break
Chandler and Joey's, continued from earlier. Chandler and Kathy are still kissing, then they stop suddenly.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no, this is bad! It's bad! This is bad!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Horrible!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait the uh, the kiss or the situation?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: No-no-no, the kiss was good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: No, but that's bad!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh! Yes! Okay! Here's what we do, we-we forget it happened.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, we-we swallow our feelings. Even if it means we're unhappy forever. Sound good?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Can you really do that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have to; he's my best friend, and you're seeing him.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Chandler, I like Joey a lot, but with you...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting) Oh-no-no-no! Don't! Don't! See-see, you're getting me confused, I'm starting to urn.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: I'm sorry. If you wanna pretend that nothing happened, I can try.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I-I think we have too.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Okay. Bye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye. (Kathy leaves and Chandler wonders over to and leans up against the door.) Are you still out there?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: (outside the door) No. (Chandler opens the door and they kiss again.)
Central Perk, Ross is performing, his music hasn't improved.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (His voice is altered to sound like a computer.) Electrifying. (He plays the sound of a ticking clock.) Emphatic time-time-time...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Rachel and Phoebe) Y'know, there's a Starbucks about three blocks down.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pushing Monica back onto the couch) It's so inspired! Look at him! Look at him go!
[cut back to Ross who finally finishes his so-called song with the same crash from before. He gets some applause, mainly 'cause he's done.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (with the altered voice) Thank you guys-guys-guys...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Hey, aren't you up next?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, I'm not playing tonight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't follow Ross! It'd be like those bicycle ridding chimps that followed The Beetles. No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, Ross sucks!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, the place has emptied because of him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, he's not even appreciated in his own time. I would give anything to not be appreciated in my own time!
Rachel and Monica look at each other, and agree on something without saying anything.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Umm, Phoebe, you suck too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, Phoebe you're... awful!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys. You suck too. (She hugs them both.)
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is opening the door, but Chandler has the chain on it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww man! I can't believe I locked myself out again! (He knocks on the door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hang on buddy! (He goes over and unlocks the door and opens it to reveal a fully furnished apartment.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (rushing in) Oh my God! What happened here? Did you do all this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I sure did.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I just thought it'd make me feel good to do something nice for my friend.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, you're amazing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no. This is amazing. (He goes over and presses a button on a remote control that opens the entertainment center doors revealing the TV.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (very excited) A TV as if it appears from nowhere! That's the dream! Man, how did you afford this stuff?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, y'know I'm 29. I mean who needs a savings account.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you are the best friends anyone has ever had.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-no-no-no, you are! You do this, you give me the great advice, and hey listen, I was thinking about what you said yesterday about focusing on one woman, I'm gonna do that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean with Casey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no, I think I'm gonna see how things go with Kathy. She's pretty cool.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Or Casey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, Kathy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could be Casey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. No, Kathy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Consider Casey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know what I think? I think somebody's got a little crush on Casey. How 'bout I fix you two up? What do you think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That all the pieces of my life are falling right into place!
Central Perk, Ross has just finished playing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! You were really great! You were really, really great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thanks, thanks. So Monica tells me that uh, you don't want to play anymore because me and y'know my talent. Is that true?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, kinda. Yeah. Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I-I can't-I mean y'know I was trying to be really y'know okay and upbeat about it, I just-I feel so dwarfed by your musical gift. I...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: See but, Pheebs that-that is the exact opposite intent of my music. Y'know my music is-is meant to inspire, and if it bothers you this much, then I... I won't play anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no. No-no-no, don't do that! How could I live with myself if I knew I was depriving the world of your music.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, okay.
Central Perk, Rachel and Monica are sitting on the couch and Phoebe is getting coffee as Chandler enters. Ross is also there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Chandler! Saw the new furniture. Very nice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! Joey has the best boyfriend ever!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I kissed Kathy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Does Joey know?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. Is there anyway, anyway you think he'll understand this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You obviously haven't screwed over a lot of your friends. (They all look at her) Which we all appreciate.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No the-the sad thing is, if you had told him how you felt before you kissed her, knowing Joey, he probably just would've just stepped aside.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, don't say that! Don't say that. That's not true. Is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think maybe, yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He loves you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Then why didn't you tell me to do that?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I said-I said something to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! No, that's right. And I thought it was a really good idea.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I remember that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I remember you did.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: God!! (Sits down in disgust.) What am I gonna do?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Chandler, you're gonna have to tell him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?! Why do I have to tell him?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because you do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, would it be okay if I wrote a song about this.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is watching TV as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Samboucha Margarita?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that a real thing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, we only had samboucha, so it is now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen ah, Joe, I-I need to, I need to talk to you about something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's-it's about Kathy. Umm, uh, I like her. I like her a lot actually.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, you're timing couldn't be better. She's not my girlfriend anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, she broke up with me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh uh, when?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Just now, after acting class. At first I thought she was doing some kind of scene, that's why I let people watch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh man, I am so sorry. Are, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I've been better. But, I'm all right. So you like her huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but I-I uh, don't have too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no, no it's uh, it's okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. You know why? 'Cause you came to me first.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I thought that would be the best thing to do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But hey, listen just so you know, you might have you're work cut out for you. 'Cause when I talked to her, I kinda got the feeling that she's into some other guy. So...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See uh, that's-that's actually what I wanted to talk to you about. I-I think I know who the other guy is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's me. I'm the other guy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I mean when you were late last night, Kathy and I got to talking, and one thing to another and...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And what?! Did you sleep with her?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! No! No! I just kissed her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!! That's even worse!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How is that worse?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know! But it's the same!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I'm sorry! But there's nothing I can do, I think I'm in love with her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who cares?! You went behind my back! I would never do that to you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're right, I have no excuses! I was totally over the line.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Over the line?! You-you're-you're so far past the line, that you-you can't even see the line! The line is a dot to you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Yes! Right! And I feel horrible. You have to believe me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is that why you bought all this stuff?! (Chandler makes a face like "Well, kinda.") Well, y'know what I will not watch your TV, I will not listen to your stereo, and there's a cinnamon raisin loaf in the new bread maker that I'm not gonna eat! You know why?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Probably because...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because it's all tainted with your betrayal. From now on this apartment is empty for me! And I'm not happy about you either. (The bread maker dings) Oh, and just so you know, I made that bread for you. (Joey walks into his bedroom and slams the door.)
Closing Credits
Central Perk, Ross is playing his music. Monica, Phoebe, and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, he's lost it. He's totally lost it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (removing ear plugs) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, his music could not get any worse. There are rats in the basement that are hanging themselves.
Ross finally finishes with the same crash, and gets some applause.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, thanks. (Sits down next to the girls) Yeah, I lost it. Y'know, I'm not gonna play anymore, (to Phoebe) would you, can you finish my set?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: After that? Yeah! No, I mean if I can help.
Phoebe gets up and goes to play, Ross goes over and sits down next to Monica and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, like I could lose it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I played bad on purpose guys.
Both Monica and Rachel laugh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so you were trying to play bad this whole time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah-no, just that last song.
End
